use_codes.desc: ACUTE TREATMENT OF INTERMITTENT, STEREOTYPIC EPISODES OF FREQUENT SEIZURE ACTIVITY (I.E., SEIZURE CLUSTERS, ACUTE REPETITIVE SEIZURES) THAT ARE DISTINCT FROM A PATIENT'S USUAL SEIZURE PATTERN IN PATIENTS WITH EPILEPSY 12 YEARS OF AGE AND OLDER

indications.text:
NAYZILAM is indicated for the acute treatment of intermittent, stereotypic episodes of
frequent seizure activity (i.e., seizure clusters, acute repetitive seizures) that are distinct from
a patient’s usual seizure pattern in patients with epilepsy 12 years of age and older.

2 DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION 8.5 Geriatric Use
2.1 Instructions Prior to Dosing 8.6 Renal Impairment
2.2 Dosage Information 8.7 Congestive Heart Failure
3 DOSAGE FORMS AND STRENGTHS 9 DRUG ABUSE AND DEPENDENCE

NAYZILAM is indicated for the acute treatment of intermittent, stereotypic episodes of
frequent seizure activity (i.e., seizure clusters, acute repetitive seizures) that are distinct from
a patient’s usual seizure pattern in patients with epilepsy 12 years of age and older.
